calendar.matters:
Hearing On Petitioner'S Writ Of Mandate (Per Court'S Order At 11/22/04 Hearing)
Off Calendar Mar-01-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Mitchell J. Green, Esq., 510-849-8686 for petitioner and moving party Lucrecia R. Cobar, R.N.
Joshua A. Room, Deputy Attorney General, 415-703-1299 for respondent and opposing party Board of Registered Nursing

RULING:
A R G U E D; Petitioner Lucrecia Cobar's writ of mandate (per court's order at 11/22/04 hearing) is granted. The Administrative record does not support the respondent's conclusions. The Court returns this case to the Nursing Board. The Nursing Board is directed to explore the various alternatives available. Respondent requested a CCP 632 statement of decision; the court will review the statute. Prevailing party to submit form of order. (301/JLW/EH) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558

Hearing On Petitioner'S Writ Of Mandate (Per Court'S Order At 11/22/04 Hearing)
Off Calendar Jan-12-2005 Continued To Mar-01-05 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301

Petitioner'S Application For Stay
Off Calendar Nov-22-2004 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Mitchell J. Green, Esq. (510) 849 8686 for Petitioner and Moving Party Cobar
Joshua Room, Deputy Attorney General (415) 703 1299 for Resp
RULING:
A R G U E D; PETITIONER LUCRECIA R. COBAR'S APPLICATION FOR STAY is taken UNDER SUBMISSION. Attorney Room submits proposed form of order; Attorney Green to submit a proposed form of order. Counsel request, if appropriate, that the hearing on the Writ of Mandate be set for hearing on 01/20/05. (301/JLW/EH) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558
